questions.question:
A rectangular paper of 20 cm x 8 cm is folded 3 times. Each fold is made along the line of symmetry, which is perpendicular to its long edge. The perimeter of the final folded sheet (in cm) is

A. 18
B. 24
C. 20
D. 21
questions.to_find: Perimeter of the paper after 3 successive folds, each along the line of symmetry perpendicular to the current long edge

understand.plain: Each fold is made along the midline that is perpendicular to whichever edge is currently the longer one. When the 20 cm length is folded twice it becomes 5 cm, which is now shorter than 8 cm, so the third fold is made perpendicular to the 8 cm (now longer) edge, halving it to 4 cm. The final sheet is 5 cm x 4 cm, giving perimeter 18 cm.

keywords.term: Line of symmetry perpendicular to the long edge
keywords.explain: A fold crease that bisects the sheet at right angles to whichever side is currently the longer one, halving that dimension.
keywords.example: For a 10 x 8 sheet the long edge is 10 cm, so the fold crease runs across the 10 cm direction, producing a 5 x 8 sheet.

keywords.explain: 2*(length + width) — the total length of all four sides.
keywords.example: A 5 x 4 sheet has perimeter 2*(5+4) = 18 cm.

keywords.explain: Each successive fold uses the dimensions of the already-folded sheet, not the original sheet, so the 'long edge' can change after a fold.
keywords.example: After folds 1 and 2 the sheet is 5 x 8; the long edge is now 8 cm, so fold 3 acts perpendicular to that edge.

understand.visual_alt: Three successive folds reduce 20x8 to 10x8 to 5x8 and then 5x4, whose perimeter is 18 cm.

given.aim: Find the perimeter of the paper after all three folds

terms.example: The paper is longest along the 20 cm direction before any fold.
terms.connects: Determines which edge is 'long' for the first fold.

terms.meaning: Each fold is made along the midline perpendicular to the current long edge, halving that dimension.
terms.example: For a 10 x 8 sheet, the fold crease is perpendicular to the 10 cm side, producing a 5 x 8 sheet.
terms.connects: Applied three times to get the final folded dimensions.

terms.example: Fold 1 gives 10x8, Fold 2 gives 5x8, Fold 3 gives 5x4.
terms.connects: After Fold 2 the long edge changes from 5 cm to 8 cm, redirecting Fold 3.
Applied to the final folded dimensions to get the answer.

given.plan: Track the sheet dimensions fold by fold, noting when the long edge changes, then apply the perimeter formula to the final folded sheet.

steps.title: Fold 1 — along 20 cm midline
steps.formula_raw: new length = 20/2 = 10 cm; width unchanged = 8 cm
steps.apply: Sheet becomes 10 cm x 8 cm. Long edge is still 10 cm.
steps.note: The fold crease is vertical, bisecting the 20 cm dimension.

steps.title: Fold 2 — along 10 cm midline
steps.formula_raw: new length = 10/2 = 5 cm; width unchanged = 8 cm
steps.apply: Sheet becomes 5 cm x 8 cm. Now 8 cm > 5 cm, so the long edge has shifted to the 8 cm side.
steps.note: This is the key insight — the 'long edge' has changed after this fold.

steps.title: Fold 3 — along 8 cm midline (now the long edge)
steps.formula_raw: new width = 8/2 = 4 cm; length unchanged = 5 cm
steps.note: Because the 8 cm side is now longer, fold 3 halves the 8 cm dimension, not the 5 cm dimension.

steps.title: Compute perimeter of final folded sheet

questions.formula_note: No standard formula-registry entries are needed; the only formula used is the rectangle perimeter P = 2*(l+w), applied directly.

questions.question:
A square paper, shown in figure (I), is folded along the dotted lines as shown in the figures (II) and (III). Then a few cuts are made as shown in figure (IV). Which one of the following patterns will be obtained when the paper is unfolded?

Note: The figures shown are representative.

A. Pattern with small filled squares at top-left and bottom-right corners and bracket-shaped marks at top-right and bottom-left corners
B. Pattern with diamond shapes at top-left and bottom-right corners and bracket-shaped marks at top-right and bottom-left corners
C. Pattern with small filled squares at top-left and bottom-right, and at top-right, with bracket-shaped marks at bottom-left and bottom corners
D. Pattern with a single central filled square and bracket-shaped marks at corners
questions.answer: A. Pattern with small filled squares at top-left and bottom-right corners and bracket-shaped marks at top-right and bottom-left corners
questions.to_find: The unfolded pattern after two diagonal/line folds and corner cuts on a square paper

understand.plain: The square is first folded so its corners meet (each fold creates a triangular or smaller square shape). Cuts made on the folded shape are replicated symmetrically when the paper is unfolded because each cut layer maps to an equivalent position on the original square. The correct unfolded pattern shows marks that are mirror images across both fold axes simultaneously.

keywords.explain: When a folded paper is cut, each cut through multiple layers produces a symmetric pattern upon unfolding — the cut repeats at every corresponding position mapped by the fold.
keywords.example: A single cut at the corner of a twice-folded square produces four symmetric holes when unfolded.

keywords.explain: The line along which the paper is folded; marks on one side are mirrored about this axis to the other side when the paper is unfolded.
keywords.example: Folding along a diagonal then cutting a notch at one edge produces a notch on the opposite edge when unfolded.

keywords.explain: Each layer of the folded paper corresponds to a specific region of the original paper; a cut through all layers creates marks at all those corresponding regions simultaneously.
keywords.example: After two folds of a square, cutting one corner of the folded triangle creates marks at all four corners when unfolded.

x='400' y='72' class='lbl ink'>→</text><rect x='420' y='30' width='80' height='80' class='bg'/><rect x='420' y='30' width='10' height='10' class='ink'/><rect x='490' y='100' width='10' height='10' class='ink'/><polyline points='490,30 500,30 500,40' fill='none' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><polyline points='420,100 420,110 430,110' fill='none' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><text x='460' y='125' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>Unfolded (A)</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: The square is folded twice to create a small triangle, cuts are made, and unfolding reveals a symmetric pattern with filled marks at opposite corners and bracket shapes at the other two corners.

given.aim: Identify the correct unfolded pattern after two folds and specific cuts on a square paper

terms.meaning: The original square paper before any fold.
terms.example: A flat square sheet; all four corners are unmarked.
terms.connects: The starting state that must be reconstructed after unfolding.

terms.meaning: Two successive folds along dotted lines — first diagonally or along a midline, then again — reducing the square to a smaller triangular or square shape.
terms.example: Folding a square along its diagonal gives a right triangle; folding that triangle along its altitude gives a smaller triangle.
terms.connects: The fold axes determine where cuts will be mirrored upon unfolding.

terms.meaning: Cuts made on the fully-folded shape — specifically corner or edge cuts that pass through multiple layers.
terms.example: A small rectangular notch cut at one corner of the folded triangle will produce marks at all symmetrically equivalent positions.
terms.connects: The number and position of cut marks in the unfolded pattern depends on how many layers each cut passes through.

terms.meaning: The correct option must reflect the fold symmetry: marks appear in positions that are mirror images of the cut positions reflected through both fold axes.
terms.example: Option A shows small filled squares (from the cut) at two diagonally opposite corners and bracket shapes at the other two, consistent with two-fold symmetry.
terms.connects: Eliminates options B, C, D which show inconsistent or wrongly placed mark shapes.

given.plan: Trace each cut through the fold layers back to its position on the original unfolded square, identifying which corners/edges carry each type of mark, and match to the correct answer option.

steps.title: Identify fold sequence from figures (I)-(III)
steps.formula_raw: Fold 1: along a diagonal, creating a right triangle. Fold 2: along the median of the triangle, creating a smaller triangle.
steps.apply: After two folds the paper is a right triangle that is one-quarter of the original square. Each point of this small triangle corresponds to multiple points on the original square.
steps.note: The corner at the right-angle vertex of the final folded shape corresponds to the centre of the original square; the acute corners correspond to corners of the original square.

steps.title: Map the cuts in figure (IV) to positions on the folded shape
steps.formula_raw: Cut 1: small square notch at one acute corner of the folded triangle. Cut 2: L-shaped bracket cut at the other acute corner.
steps.apply: Cut 1 passes through 2 layers (due to fold symmetry); it maps to 2 corner positions on the original square. Cut 2 similarly maps to 2 corner positions — the remaining two corners.
steps.note: The small filled square cut appears at two diagonally opposite corners; the bracket-shaped cut appears at the other two diagonally opposite corners.

steps.title: Compare to answer options
steps.apply: Option A shows: small filled squares at top-left and bottom-right corners, bracket (L-shape) marks at top-right and bottom-left corners. This is exactly consistent with the symmetric mapping of two types of cuts made at the two acute corners of the twice-folded triangle.
steps.note: Options B, C, and D show diamond shapes, misplaced marks, or central marks that do not correspond to the fold-and-cut geometry.

solution.result: Option A — filled squares at two diagonally opposite corners and bracket shapes at the other two corners

questions.formula_note: No algebraic formulas are needed; the solution relies entirely on spatial reasoning about fold symmetry and layer correspondence.
